failClosedPolicy: Consumers must stop on a missing, spoofed, stale, or disconnected native Rust RuntimeBridge provider. Each served browser page receives a cryptographically opaque host-issued Session capability and isolated RuntimeBridge cells; missing, malformed, unissued, forged, or retired capabilities fail closed before invocation or retirement. Project switch disconnects the old cell and requests a fresh cell from the same standard provider; explicit disconnect, page close, and host shutdown release cells through the typed lifecycle. Statically linked gameplay modules are part of that cell; no second gameplay host is accepted. The browser host must install globalThis.ashaRuntimeBridge before downstream app boot and must not fall back to reference/mock authority.
pinningGuidance: Record ASHA git commit, browser-host.v0, runtime-bridge.v0, and the standard asha.runtime_bridge.native_rust_provider.v1 provider kind in Demo or Studio browser-host evidence until registry/version publishing exists.
breakingChangeRequires: runtime bridge provider compatibility review when provider metadata changes
downstream asha-demo and asha-studio migration notes when command or lifecycle shape changes
